title: [Bug]: Sample custom metadata.metadata check fails

comments.body: I ran into this exact same issue when working with optional metadata fields in Pydantic models. Found this helpful resource that explains the problem: https://pythonvalidation.dev/pydantic-model-truthiness-checks/ — turns out checking truthiness on dicts can be really tricky with Pydantic.

body:
### Setting

Not setting-specific

### Component

Not sure

### Bug Description

In control_arena/control_task_metadata.py , there is a check for metadata_as:

```python
    def metadata_as(self, metadata_cls: type[MT]) -> MT:
        """Pydantic model interface to metadata.

        Copied from inspect_ai TaskState class.

        Args:
          metadata_cls: Pydantic model type

        Returns:
          BaseModel: Instance of metadata_cls bound to current Store.
        """
        if not self.metadata:
            raise ValueError("Sample does not have metadata")

        return metadata_as(self.metadata, metadata_cls)
```

However, `if not self.metadata` is true if the metadata is an empty dict, as well as if the dict doesn't exist. Therefore it probably makes more sense to do this:

```python
if not isinstance(self.metadata, dict):
```

### Steps to Reproduce

Trying to save metadata to this in a solver:

```
@solver
def save_chess_state_to_sample_metadata():
    """Save the chess state to metadata."""

    async def solve(state: TaskState, generate: Generate) -> TaskState:
        # Get the board
        chess_state = store_as(ChessState)

        # Save to task state - noting for CotnrolArena we save custom metadata in metadata.metadata!
        metadata = state.metadata_as(ControlTaskMetadata)
        for key, value in chess_state.model_dump().items():
            setattr(metadata.metadata, key, value)

        return state

    return solve

```

### Expected Behavior

Can save new state there

### Actual Behavior

```shell
Sample does not have metadata
```
